backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor implementing lease listing CRUD operations, user profile management, and role-based access control with admin-only permissions for creating, updating, and deleting listings.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting three implemented features for LeaseListings.tsx: proper display of lease data with partial codes and availability styling, copy-to-clipboard functionality with disabled state for unavailable leases, and empty state handling.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Comprehensive summaries of all frontend components, pages, and hooks describing routing, authentication, lease management UI, informational sections, and React Query integration.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for light and dark themes using OKLCH color space, defining design tokens for backgrounds, foregrounds, borders, and component colors.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React application with root div and module script reference to main.tsx.
frontend/src/App.tsx: TanStack Router configuration defining root layout route with child routes for homepage (/) and admin dashboard (/admin).
frontend/src/components/AdminLeaseListTa: Admin table component with inline editing for lease listings (nickname, lease code, split ratio, availability), delete functionality with confirmation dialog, loading states, and toast notifications for success/error feedback.
Authentication button handling Internet Identity login/logout with admin verification, automatic navigation to dashboard after successful admin check, and loading states during authentication flow.
frontend/src/components/AppDownloadButto: Download buttons for iOS and Android Unity apps with platform-specific icons and external links to App Store and Google Play.
Form component for creating new lease listings with nickname, UUID lease code, and split ratio fields. Validates inputs and uses mutation hook to submit data.
frontend/src/components/FAQSection.tsx: Displays an accordion-style FAQ section with six common questions about Unity lease codes, rewards, compatibility, and usage.
frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx: Footer component with three columns (about, quick links, social media), copyright notice, and caffeine.ai attribution.
Renders a grid of cards defining key Unity ecosystem terms like UNO, ULO, lease code, split ratio, and availability status.
frontend/src/components/Header.tsx: Sticky header with Unity logo, title, and admin-only button; navigates to home on logo click.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Root layout component wrapping Header, main content outlet, and Footer in a flex column structure.
frontend/src/components/LeaseListings.ts: Public-facing component displaying available lease listings in a grid with partial code display, split ratio, availability badge, and copy-to-clipboard functionality. Explicitly excludes nickname field from display.
frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.t: Authentication wrapper that verifies Internet Identity login and admin status before rendering children; redirects to home with error toast if unauthorized.
frontend/src/components/RequirementsSect: Displays two requirement cards (compatible phone and internet connection) needed to participate in Unity leasing.
frontend/src/components/RewardsSection.t: Displays a section explaining Unity's reward structure with two cards: one describing the general 75% ULO reward split and another explaining customized lease splits (e.g., 60/40).
frontend/src/components/SetupGuideSectio: Renders a three-step setup guide section with numbered cards explaining how to download the Unity app, claim a lease code, and enter the code to start earning.
frontend/src/components/TasksSection.tsx: Displays a section with three cards outlining key ULO tasks: verification, monitoring (keeping the app running), and maintenance (ensuring device connectivity).
frontend/src/components/TutorialsSection: Renders a video tutorials section with an embedded YouTube iframe showing an APK installation tutorial for Unity SDK.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and manages an authenticated or anonymous backend actor using React Query, handling identity changes and initializing access control with an optional admin token.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides Internet Identity authentication context and hooks for managing user login/logout state, identity delegation, and AuthClient lifecycle with configurable options.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Exports React Query hooks for fetching lease listings, checking admin status, managing user profiles, and performing CRUD operations on lease listings via the backend actor.
frontend/src/index.css: Defines Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for light and dark theme colors, typography styles, and design tokens for UI components.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that sets up React Query and Internet Identity providers, then renders the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.tsx: Protected admin page that displays a dashboard with a create lease form and admin lease list table, wrapped in authentication protection.
frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx: Main landing page component that renders hero section with ULO lease information, app download buttons, and multiple informational sections including lease listings, setup guide, requirements, tasks, rewards, tutorials, FAQ, and glossary.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility module for extracting and managing URL parameters from both regular query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage persistence support and functions to clear sensitive parameters from the URL.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining custom color scheme using OKLCH color space, extended spacing/shadows, container settings, and custom animations for accordion components with typography and container query plugins.
project_state.json: Project state file documenting the ULO lease marketplace architecture with admin dashboard, public frontend, and a known issue where lease listings aren't displaying despite backend data existing, with pending fix request to resolve the public query method.
